title: Add goto() timeout with Promise.race() backstop
description:
## Problem
`await bot.pathfinder.goto(...)` can hang silently when the bot is obstructed by unbreakable blocks or encounters certain edge cases. The promise never resolves and no event fires. The current `index.js` has no timeout on any `goto()` call.

## Source
Council review DEF-PAPER-3 (Confidence: 95%). The paper's recommendation to wire `path_update` for `noPath`/`timeout`/`partial` is correct but insufficient alone ŌĆö the known hang case produces _no event at all_.

## Requirements
1. Add a per-`goto()` timeout wrapper using `Promise.race()` in `MineflayerAdapter/index.js`
2. Declare a named constant for the timeout duration (e.g., `const GOTO_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000;` at the top of the file per AGENTS.md conventions)
3. When timeout fires, emit a typed event (`gotoTimeout`) with the target coordinates and duration
4. Wire `path_update` listener for `noPath`/`timeout`/`partial` status events ŌĆö forward only on non-`success` statuses plus `goal_reached` (per OQ-2 recommendation)
5. Add C# `WorldEvent` subtypes: `GotoTimeoutEvent` and `PathUpdateEvent`
6. Add parsing in `WebSocketBridge.cs` for both new events
7. Add handling in `AgentBackgroundService.cs` `ProcessEventsAsync` ŌĆö `GotoTimeoutEvent` should set OutcomeType.Unreachable on the correlated action

## Design Notes
- The `path_update` listener alone is NOT sufficient for the hang case (DEF-PAPER-3 finding)
- The `Promise.race()` is the hard backstop; `path_update` is the fast path for detectable failures
- Only forward `path_update` on status transitions (non-`success`), not every recalculation

## References
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` ŌĆö all `await bot.pathfinder.goto(...)` call sites
- Council review: DEF-PAPER-3 ŌĆö "The known hang case produces no event at all"
- Council review: OQ-2 ŌĆö "Recommend only forward on non-success statuses plus goal_reached"
- GitHub issue #222, #273 on mineflayer-pathfinder
